steps.title: Recognize the consistent format of the subsequent examples
steps.content: The examples that follow are formatted identically: ; in Iceland, the Reykjanes plant's dry steam system and ; in the US, the McGinness Hills plant's binary cycle system. Each begins with a location modifier (in Iceland, in the US) followed by a comma before the plant name. For consistency, the first example should follow the same structure: in Indonesia, the Sarulla plant.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for introducing a series of examples
steps.content: A colon introduces a list or series of examples and belongs directly after the main noun. The structure technologies: in Indonesia, [plant]; in Iceland, [plant]; and in the US, [plant] places the colon after the main noun and keeps all three examples parallel. The other options either create a comma splice (joining two independent clauses with just a comma), misleadingly narrow the scope to Indonesia, or improperly use a semicolon that breaks the connection between noun and examples. That matches choice B — B is correct.
